mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals robust economic momentum that triggers immediate algorithmic buying pressure, pushing S&P 500 returns positive within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where payroll growth exceeds the 200,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of cases when industrial production declines alongside the specified threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside a stable federal funds rate indicates monetary policy is failing to support the labor market, triggering rapid risk-off selling that depresses S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 5-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate reflects improving labor market efficiency and rising household income, which systematically supports corporate earnings and generates positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of unemployment decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized, consumer demand weakens sufficiently to trigger earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where annualized GDP growth drops below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signal immediate liquidity stress in manufacturing sectors, forcing rapid portfolio deleveraging and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when industrial production declines during periods of elevated federal funds rates.
